Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "compliance inquiry" or "hazmat" (or similar) using search_emails to find the PHMSA inquiry letter from linda.castellano@dot.gov (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187), Marcus Thorne's forwarding to Chloe flagging $59,017/violation/day exposure, Marcus's formal response to DOT identifying four shipments in scope with one documentation gap (Keiko Tanaka placarding cert), Linda Castellano's extension granting a revised deadline of June 5, 2026, and Marcus's follow-up to Chloe noting signed originals or certified copies are required.
OE 2: Search emails with a query related to "training" or "hazmat" or "coordinator" (or similar) using search_emails to find Chloe Vance's training invite email (from chloe.vance@moveops.com, cc elena.rostova@moveops.com) scheduling "Hazmat Documentation Requirements - Mandatory Training" for Thursday April 24, 2026, 2:00–2:30 PM Pacific, with required attendees: Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Blessing Okafor, Javier Morales, Suki Patel, and Marcus Thorne.
OE 3: Search Slack with a query related to "DOT" or "hazmat" or "compliance" or "PHMSA" or "placarding" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find: Marcus Thorne's financial exposure alert in #finance (C005) requesting a $50K accrual from Hana Kim; Hana's confirmation of the $50,000 accrual (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, due June 5); and Marcus's update in #finance that the extension to June 5 was granted with the $50K accrual staying on the books.
OE 4: Search Slack with a query related to "DOT" or "placarding" or "documentation status" or "hazmat" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages or retrieve #operations (C006) history using conversations_history to find: Chloe's documentation status for all 4 moves (Zhao COMPLETE, Tanaka INCOMPLETE - missing placarding cert, Patel COMPLETE, Reyes COMPLETE - not hazmat, overweight/oversize); Blessing Okafor's admission she relied on verbal confirmation from the Swift driver for the Tanaka placarding and never obtained the signed certificate; and Chloe's update that Greg Pallone at Swift confirmed the signed cert exists in their archive (job #SR-2026-04-1538, driver Mike Becerra, arriving within 24 hours).
OE 5: Search Slack for Elena Rostova's hazmat policy in #executive (C004) and #general (C001) using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history to find her mandatory "Hazmat Documentation Requirement" policy - a 4-item DOT Documentation Checklist (shipping papers, placarding certificate, carrier certification, emergency response docs) required before any hazmat move can be marked Completed or invoiced. Chloe owns implementation and training; Marcus owns the invoicing gate.
OE 6: Search Slack in #operations (C006) for Chloe Vance's implementation plan using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history - checklist template creation, Airtable "DOT Docs Complete" gate, and training scheduled for April 24 at 2:00 PM Pacific with all four coordinators tagged.
OE 7: Search or list Linear projects and issues with a query related to "DOT" or "compliance" (or similar) using linear_list_projects and/or linear_list_issues to find the "DOT Compliance Audit" project (project_dot_compliance_audit, lead: Chloe Vance, team: Operations) and its 5 issues: OPS-1 Zhao/GreenStack/Class 9 Lithium - Done; OPS-2 Tanaka/Mosaic/Class 3B Laser - In Progress; OPS-3 Patel/NorthWind/Lithium UPS - Done; OPS-4 Reyes/GreenStack/Oversize Rig - Done (not hazmat); OPS-5 Obtain retroactive placarding cert from Swift - Todo/Urgent.
OE 8: Read Linear comments on issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind (OPS-3) using linear_list_comments to discover the documentation integrity problem: the ticket is marked Done but no signed chain-of-custody log can be located, and the Airtable record for Raj Patel still shows Status = In Progress - a systems mismatch where Linear implies documentation is complete while Airtable implies the move is still open.
OE 9: Search QuickBooks for DOT-related bills using quickbooks_search_bills to find Marcus Thorne's $50,000 contingent liability accrual (bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2, DocNumber: ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, vendor: U.S. DOT - PHMSA, due date: June 5, 2026), referencing Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187 and citing the Keiko Tanaka placarding gap as the base case for the penalty under 49 CFR 107.329.
OE 10: Search or list Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records (field_name "Special Requirements", value "hazmat" or "DOT" or similar) or airtable_list_records (base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Relocations") to find and verify the 4 hazmat-flagged moves: Mei-Lin Zhao (GreenStack, Houston→Portland, Class 9 lithium, In Progress), Keiko Tanaka (Mosaic, Columbus→Seattle, Class 3B laser, In Progress), Raj Patel (NorthWind, Chicago→Denver, lithium UPS, In Progress - physical move done but record not updated), Joaquín Reyes (GreenStack, Phoenix→Seattle, 900-lb solar rig, In Progress - not hazmat).
OE 11: Search calendar for events related to "hazmat" or "training" or "DOT" (or similar) using calendar_search_events to verify whether Chloe's mandatory coordinator training session (April 24, 2:00 PM Pacific) has a corresponding calendar event and whether it occurred.
OE 12: Look up Elena Rostova's and Priya Chakrabarti's contact information using contacts_search_contacts to confirm email addresses (elena.rostova@moveops.com, priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com) before sending the status report.
OE 13: Send an email (via send_email) from priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com with a comprehensive DOT compliance status report covering: documentation status for all 4 shipments (3 complete, Tanaka pending Swift cert); the Tanaka placarding gap and resolution status; the Raj Patel documentation integrity issue (Linear Done vs Airtable In Progress, missing signed custody log); Marcus's $50K accrual; coordinator training status (April 24 session); Elena's policy implementation progress; Reyes classification (not hazmat, overweight/oversize); and remaining action items before the June 5 submission deadline.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

2 The email content to Elena (visible in send_email parameters) identifies the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics placarding certificate as the critical open documentation gap.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The email body has an entire section labeled 'MOVE 2: Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics' marked as '⚠️ CRITICAL', and dedicates extensive detail to the placarding certificate gap, calling it 'THE SINGLE BIGGEST RISK ITEM.' This criterion is fully met.
✓Run #3The email content extensively discusses the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics placarding certificate gap, identifying it as 'the single blocking item for the entire submission' and describing that MoveOps has no confirmation of receipt despite Greg Pallone promising it. This is clearly identified as the critical open documentation gap.
✓Run #4The email content explicitly identifies 'Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics' placarding certificate as the critical open gap, with detailed discussion in Move 2 section and labeling it as 'BLOCKING' and the 'single document that determines whether MoveOps faces a $50K+ fine.'
✓Run #5The email has an extensive section on the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics placarding certificate, describing it as the 'SINGLE BIGGEST RISK ITEM' and detailing the missing signed certificate from Swift.
✓Run #6The email content extensively discusses the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics placarding certificate as the critical open documentation gap, dedicating an entire section (MOVE 2) to it and marking it as 'CRITICAL — NOT CONFIRMED RESOLVED'.
3 The email content to Elena mentions Marcus Thorne's $50,000 contingent liability accrual for DOT penalties (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 or similar reference).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1Section 3 of the email explicitly mentions: '$50,000 contingent liability (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001), booked April 21, due June 5' with details about Marcus directing Hana to set it up and ASC 450-20 treatment.
✓Run #2The email Section 3 explicitly lists the QuickBooks bill 'ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, $50,000, due June 5, 2026' and references the $50K contingent liability recorded by Marcus. The criterion is fully met.
✓Run #3The email content explicitly references 'ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, $50,000, due date June 5, 2026' and describes the $50,000 contingent liability accrual booked in QuickBooks by Hana Kim under Marcus's direction, including the ASC 450-20 basis and the vendor/account details.
✓Run #4The email explicitly mentions the '$50,000 contingent liability accrual' and references 'ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001' with detail about it being booked by Hana Kim and approved by Marcus.
✓Run #5The email explicitly references 'Doc Number: ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001', '$50,000 contingent liability', booked April 21, 2026, with specific reference to ASC 450-20 and the DOT case number.
✓Run #6The email explicitly references the $50,000 contingent liability accrual (document ref ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001) booked by Marcus/Hana in QuickBooks per ASC 450-20, in section 4 of the email.
4 The email body to Elena references the June 5, 2026 submission deadline.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The June 5, 2026 deadline is referenced multiple times in the email, including the subject line, Section 1 (as the extended deadline), and the closing summary ('40 days to submission... before the June 5 submission deadline').
✓Run #2The email opens with 'ahead of the June 5 submission deadline' and later mentions 'Bill recorded: ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, $50,000, due June 5, 2026' and 'June 5, 2026 (extended from original May 21).' The June 5 deadline is clearly referenced.
✓Run #3The email subject line mentions 'June 5 Submission', and the body prominently states 'DEADLINE: June 5, 2026 (extended from May 21; one-time accommodation, no further extensions).' The June 5 deadline is clearly referenced multiple times.
✓Run #4The email explicitly states 'Deadline: June 5, 2026 (40 days from today)' in the Executive Summary section, and references the June 5 submission date multiple times throughout the email.
✓Run #5The email subject line and body both reference the June 5 submission deadline multiple times (e.g., 'We have 40 days until the June 5 deadline', 'before the June 5 deadline').
✓Run #6The email explicitly references the June 5 submission deadline multiple times, including in the subject line ('40 Days to Submission Deadline'), the opening paragraph ('June 5 submission deadline'), and the accrual section noting 'due date June 5, 2026'.
5 The email body to Elena mentions the coordinator compliance training session scheduled for April 24, 2026, organized by Chloe Vance.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1Section 5 of the email explicitly states: 'Chloe announced a mandatory hazmat documentation training session for all relocation coordinators, tentatively scheduled for Thursday, April 24 at 2:00 PM Pacific.' Both the date and Chloe's role are clearly mentioned.
✓Run #2Section 5 of the email explicitly states 'Chloe scheduled a mandatory hazmat documentation training session for Thursday, April 24, 2:00–2:30 PM Pacific via Zoom.' Both the April 24 date and Chloe Vance's role are clearly identified.
✓Run #3The email explicitly references 'Chloe committed (in Slack on ~April 22) to scheduling a mandatory 30-minute training session for all coordinators on Thursday, April 24 at 2:00 PM Pacific.' This covers both Chloe Vance and the April 24 training date with full context.
✓Run #4The email's Section 4 (TRAINING) explicitly mentions Chloe scheduling 'a mandatory hazmat documentation training session for Thursday, April 24, 2:00 PM PT via Zoom' and notes there is no evidence it occurred.
✓Run #5Section 3 of the email explicitly states 'Chloe announced a mandatory hazmat documentation training session... scheduled for Thursday, April 24 at 2:00 PM Pacific' and 'She sent a calendar invite email on April 22', fully covering the April 24 session organized by Chloe.
✓Run #6The email explicitly references the April 24, 2026 training session organized by Chloe Vance in section 6, stating 'Chloe scheduled a mandatory hazmat documentation training session for Thursday, April 24 at 2:00 PM Pacific.'
6 The email content to Elena mentions that the Raj Patel / NorthWind Technologies Linear ticket (OPS-3) is marked Done but no signed chain-of-custody log can be located.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The email actually reports the opposite: for Raj Patel (Move 3), it says the placarding certificate is 'ON FILE ✓ (included in NorthWind's CISO chain-of-custody documentation)' and that the Linear ticket closure is 'Legitimate.' The criterion expected the email to flag a missing signed chain-of-custody log for Patel, but the agent found the documentation to be complete.
✗Run #2The agent actually found Raj Patel's documentation to be complete, including the chain-of-custody log ('NorthWind's CISO-mandated chain-of-custody protocol included signed placarding confirmation'). The email marks OPS-3 as DONE and the Patel shipment as fully verified. The criterion requires reporting a missing chain-of-custody log for Patel, which the agent did not find and did not report.
✗Run #3The email states Raj Patel's documentation is complete and Linear ticket #3 is marked DONE, and that the documentation is solid. It does NOT mention any missing signed chain-of-custody log for Raj Patel. The email actually says Patel has 'the strongest documentation package' with the chain-of-custody log included. There is no mention of a missing chain-of-custody log or discrepancy between ticket status and documentation for Patel.
✗Run #4The email states OPS-3 (Patel/NorthWind) is 'DONE' and 'documentation verified complete / Legitimate close,' and notes the placarding certificate is on file. It does NOT mention any missing signed chain-of-custody log for the Raj Patel shipment. The agent reported Patel's documentation as complete, not as a gap.
✗Run #5The email describes Raj Patel's documentation as SOLID and notes the chain-of-custody log IS on file: '(B) Placarding certificate — ON FILE ✓ (NorthWind's CISO-mandated chain-of-custody protocol included signed placarding confirmation)'.
